title: [lichen_l2] peer_table active flag not zeroed on startup
description:
State machine bug: The peer_table[] static array's active flags are never explicitly initialized to false. They rely on BSS zero-initialization.

While C guarantees static variables are zero-initialized, the peer_table contains pointers to pubkeys and eui64s. If the BSS initialization is ever skipped (e.g., warm restart on some bootloaders), stale peer entries with active=true could persist.

Furthermore, after lichen_l2_enable(false), the peer_table is cleared (lines 791-795). But after lichen_l2_enable(true), there's no re-zeroing if link_ctx was already initialized.

Location: lichen_l2.c:244 (static declaration), lines 791-795 (cleanup in disable)

Recommendation: Add explicit zeroing of peer_table in lichen_l2_iface_init() before any operations, or in lichen_link_init() call path.

Priority: P3 - defense-in-depth, BSS init is reliable on most platforms.
close_reason: Fixed: Added explicit memset() in lichen_l2_iface_init() to zero peer_table at boot. While C guarantees static BSS zeroing, explicit initialization is defensive and documents the invariant.
